prompt: I'm porting a desktop C++ application to an STM32H7. The original code uses std::vector, std::string, std::shared_ptr, virtual functions, exceptions, and RTTI extensively. Flash is 2MB, RAM is 1MB. What's my strategy for porting this? Give me a concrete migration guide with code examples for each replacement pattern.
assertions: Addresses all 6 features: vector, string, shared_ptr, virtual, exceptions, RTTI
Shows before (desktop) and after (embedded) code examples for at least 4 patterns
Recommends ETL (Embedded Template Library) or fixed-size alternatives for containers
Recommends CRTP as the replacement for virtual dispatch in performance-critical paths
Recommends std::unique_ptr (with custom deleter) over std::shared_ptr
